key_metrics: Time to Diagnosis / Time to Treatment

benchmarks.patient_retention: > 70% continue using the service after 90 days
benchmarks.clinician_adoption: > 50% of invited clinicians actively use within 30 days
benchmarks.data_breach_incidents: 0 — a single breach triggers HIPAA notification requirements

hipaa.description: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act — US federal law
hipaa.covered_entities: Health plans, healthcare providers, healthcare clearinghouses
hipaa.business_associates: Vendors handling PHI on behalf of covered entities must sign BAA
hipaa.phi_definition: 18 categories of identifiers linked to health data (name, DOB, address, diagnosis, etc.)

safeguards.administrative: Risk assessment, workforce training, access management policies
safeguards.physical: Facility access controls, workstation security, device controls
safeguards.technical: Access controls, audit logs, encryption, automatic logoff

hipaa.breach_notification: Report breaches affecting > 500 individuals to HHS within 60 days

hitech.description: Health Information Technology for Economic and Clinical Health Act
hitech.key_rule: Strengthens HIPAA enforcement and extends liability to business associates directly

telehealth_regulations.interstate_licensing: Providers must be licensed in the state where the patient is located
telehealth_regulations.ryan_haight_act: Federal law requiring in-person exam before prescribing controlled substances remotely (exceptions exist post-COVID)
telehealth_regulations.parity_laws: Many states require insurers to cover telehealth at the same rate as in-person care

fda.software_as_medical_device: SaMD — software intended to diagnose or treat must comply with FDA 510(k) or De Novo pathway
fda.wellness_exemption: General wellness apps (fitness, stress management) are exempt

phi_handling_rules: Never store PHI in localStorage or client-side storage
All PHI must be encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3)
Database access to PHI tables requires audit logging with user, action, timestamp, and record ID
PHI must never appear in error messages, logs, or URLs
De-identify data for analytics — remove all 18 HIPAA identifiers
Minimum necessary principle — only access the PHI required for the specific task
Sign BAA with every vendor that handles PHI (AWS, Google Cloud, Supabase if storing PHI)
Implement automatic session timeout (15 minutes idle max for clinical users)
best_practices: Get legal review before building any feature touching PHI
Sign Business Associate Agreements (BAA) with Supabase, AWS, and any analytics vendor
Design role-based access control — clinicians only see their own patient panels
Build audit logs before storing any PHI — logging is a HIPAA requirement
Telehealth features require checking provider licensing per state
Interoperability: use HL7 FHIR R4 for data exchange with EHR systems
Patient consent flows must be explicit and logged with timestamp
Build offline support carefully — cached PHI on devices creates breach risk
common_risks: HIPAA violation from inadequate PHI protection — six-figure fines per violation
Practicing medicine without a license — AI diagnosis features require careful legal framing
Interstate licensing exposure in telehealth — providers and platforms share liability
Physician burnout from bad UX — clinical tools must reduce, not increase, cognitive load
EHR integration failures — HL7/FHIR integrations are notoriously brittle
Data breach — healthcare records sell for $250+ on dark web (10x more than credit cards)
recommended_integrations: Stripe — patient billing and subscription management
Twilio — HIPAA-compliant SMS reminders and telehealth SMS
Daily.co or Zoom for Healthcare — HIPAA-compliant video consultations
Health Gorilla or Particle Health — FHIR-based EHR data access
Supabase — with BAA signed for PHI storage
